# Authorization v3 — AZ3-4.2 exact-semantics and concurrency proof transcript

Generated by `go test -run TestAZ3ProofProtocol` in `examples/auth-cms/cmd/server`.
This is a recorded artifact: each section below was OBSERVED in a real protocol run
over the auth-cms host composition (the AZ3-4.1 memstore bundle under
authzGuardianPolicy, the hostMutationGuard, and the separately held SystemMutator)
with a capturing AuditSink wired into `Config.Audit`.

No secrets: authorization carries no session tokens or password material; MutationIDs
are opaque idempotency tokens (safe to retain). This transcript claims NO effects
delivery and NO generic authorization admin API — both are deferred follow-ups.

MutationIDs in this run are derived deterministically (DeriveMutationID) so the
artifact is reproducible; actor-facing production callers mint unguessable IDs with
NewMutationID. Possession of a MutationID is never authority — the guard runs on
every actor-facing application and replay.

## 1. Ordinary member cannot self-grant

- command: Service.GrantRelationship(actor=user:ordinary, grant project:p1#owner <- user:ordinary)
- result: rejected, errors.Is(sdk.ErrForbidden)=true (guard denied before Apply)
- stored-row check: user:ordinary has manage_access on project:p1 = false (no owner row committed)
- audit: decision=denied operation=grant scope=project:p1(resource)

## 2. Authorized manager grants an exact group#member subject

- command: Service.GrantRelationship(actor=user:manager, grant doc:d2#viewer <- group:eng#member)
- receipt: outcome=applied revision=2 replayed=false schema_digest=13e967a6b71e1657…
- live schema digest: 13e967a6b71e1657… (matches receipt)
- stored viewer target(s): group:eng#member
- Check(user:alice member-of-eng, view, doc:d2) = true (member gains access)
- Check(user:stranger, view, doc:d2) = false (non-member denied)
- audit: decision=accepted operation=grant scope=doc:d2(resource) outcome=applied

## 3. A group#admin grant does not authorize an ordinary member

- command: Service.GrantRelationship(actor=user:manager, grant doc:d3#viewer <- group:eng#admin)
- receipt: outcome=applied revision=2
- Check(user:alice group:eng#member, view, doc:d3) = false (member does NOT satisfy the admin userset)
- Check(user:carol group:eng#admin, view, doc:d3) = true (admin satisfies it)
- audit: decision=accepted operation=grant scope=doc:d3(resource) outcome=applied

## 4. Decision APIs reject userset-valued callers

- structural: CheckRequest.Principal is a PrincipalRef=true; PrincipalRef has a Relation field=false (none)
- structural: the STORED subject type SubjectRef has a Relation field=true — a different type no decision path accepts
- consequence: a userset ('type:id#relation') caller cannot be expressed at any decision boundary — it is prevented by the type, never ignored at runtime
- command: Check(principal=group:eng CONCRETE, view, doc:dc) — the only group caller expressible is the concrete entity
- Check(group:eng concrete, view, doc:dc) = true (the concrete group entity itself)
- Check(user:alice member-of-eng, view, doc:dc) = false (a member is NOT the concrete group; no userset-caller path exists)

## 5. A navigational Through relation rejects userset targets at schema compile

- command: NewService(schema{ space.view = AnyOf(Direct(viewer), Through(parent, view)); space.parent allows space#member })
- result: construction FAILED, errors.Is(sdk.ErrInvalidInput)=true
- compile error: authorization schema: invalid input: schema compilation failed with 1 error(s):
  - space.parent: relation is used by a Through traversal and must contain concrete resource subjects only, but allows userset space#member

## 6. Non-self Through-root hierarchy: Check and Lookup return the same descendants

- setup: space:root inherits view ONLY from org:O#admin@user:u1 (a non-self Through root); root<-mid<-leaf via parent
- Check(user:u1, view, space:leaf) = true (grandchild of the org-derived root)
- LookupResources(user:u1, view, space).IDs = [leaf mid root]
- parity: each Lookup id is Check-allowed = true

## 7. A global role appears in effective role enumeration

- command: SystemMutator.AssignRole(user:dave, role=auditor, GLOBAL) — no direct scoped row on project:p7
- ListEffectiveRoleGrantsByResource(project:p7): user:dave/auditor present, provenance=global (direct=false global=true)
- HasRole(user:dave, auditor, project:p7) = true (scoped global fallback agrees with enumeration)

## 8. A scoped revoke while a global remains reports same_role_grant_remains

- setup: user:erin holds auditor GLOBALLY and scoped on project:p8
- command: Service.UnassignRole(actor=user:manager, user:erin/auditor scoped on project:p8)
- result: outcome=applied same_role_grant_remains=true (computed inside the atomic critical section)
- HasRole(user:erin, auditor, project:p8) after revoke = true (global fallback retains it)
- audit: decision=accepted operation=role_assign scope=project:p8(resource) outcome=applied
- audit: decision=accepted operation=role_unassign scope=project:p8(resource) outcome=applied

## 9. Two concurrent last-owner revokes produce one success / one invariant block

- setup: each of 16 rounds seeds project#owner@{o1,o2}; two goroutines concurrently revoke o1 and o2 (platform-admin actor)
- outcome tally across all rounds: applied=16 invariant_blocked=16
- invariant: every round produced exactly one applied + one invariant_blocked, with exactly one owner remaining (total remaining owners across rounds = 16 = rounds)
- run under -race: the memstore's single shared lock is the one database arbiter; the two owners can never both be removed

## 10. Stale revision and MutationID payload mismatch return stable errors

- command A: GrantRelationship(actor=user:manager, project:p10#member <- user:x, ExpectedRevision=0) against a scope already at revision >= 1
- result A: errors.Is(ErrStaleRevision)=true (a command error, never a domain outcome, nothing committed)
- command B: reuse one MutationID for two different payloads (project:p10#member <- user:y, then <- user:z)
- result B: errors.Is(ErrMutationMismatch)=true (never a silent overwrite)
- audit: decision=failed operation=grant scope=project:p10(resource) reason=stale_revision
- audit: decision=accepted operation=grant scope=project:p10(resource) outcome=applied
- audit: decision=failed operation=grant scope=project:p10(resource) reason=mutation_payload_mismatch

## 11. An exact retry returns the original outcome with Replayed=true

- command: GrantRelationship(actor=user:manager, project:p11#member <- user:frank), issued twice with the SAME MutationID and payload
- first: outcome=applied revision=2 replayed=false
- retry: outcome=applied revision=2 replayed=true (original outcome, no revision bump)
- audit: decision=accepted operation=grant scope=project:p11(resource) outcome=applied
- audit: decision=accepted operation=grant scope=project:p11(resource) outcome=applied

## 12. Resource teardown is possible only through the separately held SystemMutator with a recorded reason

- structural: actor-facing Service exposes TeardownAuthorizationScope=false; SystemMutator exposes it=true
- precondition: empty reason rejected, errors.Is(ErrTeardownReasonRequired)=true
- command: SystemMutator.TeardownAuthorizationScope(project:p12, reason=<recorded>)
- receipt: outcome=applied revision=3 (the one op allowed to remove the last owner)
- stored-row check after teardown: owner rows=0 member rows=0
- audit: decision=accepted operation=resource_teardown scope=project:p12(resource) outcome=applied detail=az3-4.2 proof: project p12 destroyed by owner request
